After dancing Stravinsky Violin Concerto with Amar Ramasar on Saturday, Krohn will return to the NYCB studios on Monday in a new role: ballet master.
We had the chance to talk to the thoughtful and eloquent dancer about her time with the company and goals for the future.

As soon as I knew I wanted to be a professional dancer, I knew that I wanted to be in New York City
I knew that I wanted

and I knew that I wanted to be at the
New York
C
tance

As soon as I knew I wanted to be a professional dancer, I knew that I wanted to be in New York City Ballet always

I moved to New York when I was 14 to train at the School of American Ballet, and I got my apprenticeship with the company when I was 17, so it was really a dream come true.
Krohn and Adrian Danchig-Waring in Balanchine Video Courtesy NYCB

What have been your favorite ballets or roles to dance?
Balanchine Stravinsky Violin Concerto,
which I
will
dance for my final show,
has always been a favorite, as well as Balanchine Movements for Piano and Orchestra

Did you always know that you wanted to stay on with the company?
It had been in the back of my mind for a number of years,
but I didn\u00b7really address it formally until a year ago.

Roy Kaiser to Become Nevada Ballet Theatre
become Nevada Ballet
Nevada Ballet

theatre's New Artistic Director
In 2014 the dance world
was surprised when longtime 

Pennsylvania
ballet artistic director Roy Kaiser stepped down

Kaiser、昨日発表されたことがわかった。フリーソリース、フリーコラテード、アマタノバ
が、 Nevada Ballet Theatre の新しい芸術監督として就任します。

ジェームズ・カnelフィールドは去りました。

NBTの46年間の歴史に初めて第四代目アーティストディレクターを務めるカイザー

The company
will be
gaining
a highly experienced lead

Following
his rise through the ranks to principal dancer at Pennsylvania Ballet, Kaiser worked as a ballet master and eventually took the reigns as the company
Pennsylvania Ballet added 90 new ballets and 35 world premieres to their repertoire under his leadership.

Roy Kaiser with Pennsylvania Ballet Dancers.
Photo by Alexander Iziliaev, Courtesy Nevada Ballet Theatre.
